figures.caption: Table 1. Attack results on three real-world agents. The number of prompts is 30 and the memory size is 300.
Figure 3. Ablation study analyzing the impacts of different factors on ADAM.
Table 7. Original query and rewritten variants produced by different LLMs, confirming that the malicious intent still persists and our attack would still work under query rewriting.
Figure 7. EQ and EE across five runs, EhrAgent, QWEN-72B. Results confirm that the proposed attack performs consistently across runs rather than due to randomness or variance in execution.
Figure 4. Embedding distribution of both ground truth (i.e., Oracle) and ADAM (Ours). The results confirm that the better distribution estimation, the closer ADAM is to Oracle performance.
Figure 5. Attack against four defenses. Pale regions indicate attack degradation after applying a defense.
Figure 1. Workflow of the proposed ADAM attack. A malicious query (left) versus a benign query (right) to the agent.
Table 16. Attack examples on EHRAgent (MIMIC-iii dataset)
Table 17. More attack examples on EHRAgent (E-ICU dataset)

Figure 6. Estimated versus ground-truth distributions for EhrAgent across attack rounds. Results confirm that the estimated distribution progressively aligns with the ground truth as the attack proceeds.
Table 11. EQ and EE of ADAM and baselines averaged across five runs. Results confirm that our attack remains substantially stronger than all baselines.
